news.headline: New York Court of Appeals rules only citizens can vote in municipal elections, overturning law legalizing non-citizen voting.
news.summary: The highest court in New York State has overturned a New York City law allowing non-citizens to vote in municipal elections. The ruling by the New York Court of Appeals on March 21, 2025, has been praised by Americans for Citizen Voting. The law, which permitted non-citizen voting, has been struck down following this decision. This will impact a significant portion of the population in the city who do not hold citizenship and were eligible to vote under the now-defunct law.

news.headline: NYMEX Diesel April futures settle at $2.2496 a gallon.
NYMEX Gasoline April futures settle at $2.1954 a gallon.
Hg aims to grow into a $100B fund focused on tech investments, according to Bloomberg.
NYMEX WTI Crude May futures settle at $68.28 a barrel, up 21 cents, 0.31 %.
NYMEX Natural Gas April futures settle at $3.9800/MMBtu.
PDD Holdings, parent company of Temu, reports 2Q miss with revenues of $15.3 billion. Share price drops post-earnings. Analysts maintain Buy ratings with targets of $160 and $157.
news.summary: PDD Holdings Inc (NASDAQ:PDD) saw its shares plummet in early trading following underwhelming fourth-quarter revenue results. The company, owner of Temu, unveiled its financial performance during an active earnings season. Notable analyst insights include Benchmark's Fawne Jiang affirming a Buy rating with a target price of $160, while Goldman Sachs' Ronald Keung retained a Buy rating with a target price of $157. Benchmark revealed that PDD's quarterly revenues stood at RMB 111 billion ($15.3 billion), marking a 24% increase year-over-year but falling short of estimates by RMB 6 billion ($827 million). The company experienced a 17% growth in online marketing services. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Windsor CAS laid off 26 workers (10% of staff) and closed Leamington Office, risking children's safety. CUPE president decries "betrayal".
news.summary: In Windsor, Ontario, on March 19, the Windsor-Essex Children’s Aid Society took a controversial decision to lay off 26 employees, which accounts for around 10 percent of their non-management workforce. This move has raised concerns among CUPE members associated with the agency who believe it could jeopardize the safety of children under their care. Moreover, the CAS plans to close its Leamington Office by the end of the month, further impacting the Essex County region. This decision has been strongly criticized by Craig Hesman, the president of CUPE 2286, who labeled it as a betrayal of the community and the organization's core purpose.

news.headline: Income Financial Trust released financial results for 2024 ending December 31st. Reports accessible on sedarplus.com and quadravest.com.
news.summary: Toronto-based company Income Financial Trust, known as Income Financial, has released its annual financial statements and management report for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024. Interested parties can access these documents online at www.sedarplus.com and on Income Financial's website at www.quadravest.com. This information is pertinent for stakeholders and investors looking to review the company's performance throughout the year.

news.headline: JBM lists The Collective at Archer in Gainesville, FL - a 172 standalone Build-to-Rent community.
news.summary: A prominent advisory firm, JBM® Institutional Multifamily Advisors, based in St. Petersburg, Florida, recently announced the exclusive listing of The Collective at Archer in Gainesville, Florida. This high-end institutional real estate asset is fully stabilized and classified as Class A+, tailored for Build-to-Rent purposes. The property consists of 172 individual standalone units, highlighting its substantial size and investment potential.

news.headline: Stock market indexes up while Nasdaq approaches positive territory, an insurance stock hits high, excluding Berkshire Hathaway. Dow Jones and Nasdaq on the rise.
news.summary: On Friday afternoon, the major stock indexes showed improvement as the Nasdaq composite entered positive territory. The Dow Jones and Nasdaq were flirting with positive territory, showing potential gains. A notable insurance stock reached a high, distinguishing itself from Berkshire Hathaway. The overall market dynamics were captured in live coverage by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: American Water rings NYSE Opening Bell on 3/21/25 for World Water Day. Largest US water utility. CEO emphasizes water resource conservation.
news.summary: In Camden, New Jersey, American Water, the leading regulated water and wastewater utility company in the U.S. with a stock ticker symbol NYSE: AWK, marked World Water Day by ringing The Opening Bell® on Friday, March 21, 2025. M. Susan Hardwick, CEO of American Water, expressed gratitude for the opportunity, stating, “World Water Day serves as a vital reminder of the need to safeguard our water sources and the critical"

news.headline: Orbia to announce Q1 2025 earnings after market close on April 24, 2025. Conference call on April 25, 2025, at 9 am MX | 11 am ET. Key speakers to include CEO, CFO, and VP Investor Relations.
news.summary: Mexico City-based Orbia Advance Corporation, S.A.B. de C.V. is set to announce its first quarter 2025 financial results post-market closure on Thursday, April 24, 2025. Following this, a conference call and Q&A session will be held by Management on Friday, April 25, 2025. The event is scheduled for 9:00 a.m. MX | 11:00 a.m. ET. The key speakers during the call will include Sameer Bharadwaj (Chief Executive Officer), Jim Kelly (Chief Financial Officer), and Diego Echave (VP, Investor Relations). For those interested, dial-in numbers will be provided for participation.

news.headline: UFC Heavyweight Champion Jon Jones becomes co-owner of Dirty Boxing Championship with "Platinum" Mike Perry and managers; aims to revolutionize combat sports.
news.summary: In a recent announcement in Miami on March 21, 2025, it was revealed that Jon Jones, the reigning UFC Heavyweight Champion, has become a co-owner of the edgy combat sports organization known as Dirty Boxing Championship (DBX). Joining him in this venture are "Platinum" Mike Perry and experienced managers Abe and Malki Kawa. Jones expressed his enthusiasm for the partnership, stating that he was captivated by DBX from its initial event. This strategic move signifies Jones' expansion into the world of combat sports beyond his UFC success.

news.headline: NGPL PipeCo LLC sells 25.0% interest in NGPL Holdings to ArcLight Capital Partners. ArcLight now holds 62.5%. Kinder Morgan still operates the company.
news.summary: NGPL PipeCo LLC shared that Brookfield Infrastructure Partners L.P. will sell its complete 25.0% minority stake in NGPL Holdings LLC to ArcLight Capital Partners, LLC. Upon completion, ArcLight funds will acquire a 62.5% interest in NGPL Holdings. Kinder Morgan, Inc. remains the operator of PipeCo's pipeline.

news.headline: SBB Research Group Foundation sponsors Automotive Mentoring Group in Chicago, helping end gang violence, offering mentorship in auto repair.
news.summary: In Chicago, the Automotive Mentoring Group recently joined forces with the SBB Research Group Foundation to support local nonprofits through the Champion A Charity Program. The AMG focuses on eliminating gang violence and empowering Chicago residents, offering mentorship and teaching automotive repair skills. Their goal is to provide a pathway for individuals to enter the automotive industry. This partnership marks a collaborative effort between AMG and SBB Research Group Foundation in their volunteer initiative, supporting the community and providing valuable opportunities.

news.headline: U.S. Air Force chooses Boeing to build next-gen fighter aircraft, Next-Generation Air Dominance (NGAD) Platform.
news.summary: In Arlington, Virginia on March 21, 2025, the U.S. Air Force disclosed that Boeing (NYSE: BA) has secured a contract for the development, construction, and delivery of the next-generation fighter aircraft. This project, titled Next-Generation Air Dominance (NGAD) Platform, signals the arrival of a cutting-edge era for the United States.

news.headline: Coinbase in final talks to acquire Deribit, a derivatives platform, according to Bloomberg.
Accelerate Diagnostics submits WAVE System and gram-negative blood culture menu to FDA for 510(k) clearance.NASDAQ:AXDX.
news.summary: A company in Tucson, Arizona, Accelerate Diagnostics, Inc., known for its rapid in vitro diagnostics for microbiology, has disclosed that it has officially submitted its Accelerate WAVE™ system and test kit for positive blood culture gram-negative to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on March 21, 2025. This step is significant for the company listed on Nasdaq as AXDX.

news.headline: NASA astronauts Anne McClain and Nichole Ayers will answer STEM-related questions from students in New York.
news.summary: In Washington on March 21, 2025, it was announced that students hailing from Richmond Hill, New York, will be given an exclusive opportunity to interact with NASA astronauts Anne McClain and Nichole Ayers. The astronauts will be addressing pre-recorded questions pertaining to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. This exchange will take place while McClain and Ayers are aboard a spacecraft.

news.headline: Cardiac biomarkers market set to hit $10.08 billion by 2031 per Meticulous Research® report.
news.summary: In Redding, California on March 21, 2025, a recent market research report was released on the Cardiac Biomarkers Market. The report covers various types of biomarkers such as Troponin, CK-MB, Myoglobin, and BNP, along with applications like Myocardial Infarction and Congestive Heart Failure. The report provides insights into market size, share, forecasts, and trends analysis. It also discusses the diverse end-users of these cardiac biomarkers. This comprehensive analysis aims to shed light on the evolving landscape of cardiac biomarkers, catering to the increasing demand in the healthcare industry.

news.headline: Heathrow closure highlights rapid chaos escalation for airlines and airports; requires careful response management.
news.summary: During times of crisis, airline network operation centers ramp up their efforts to effectively manage the response. It requires a delicate balance to coordinate activities and ensure smooth operations. These centers play a vital role in the airline industry by overseeing flight schedules, communication with crew and passengers, and implementing necessary changes to adapt to the situation. The coordination of resources and teams within these centers is crucial, as they handle a high volume of data and decisions - often managing hundreds of flights with thousands of passengers daily. The efficiency and effectiveness of these operation centers are essential in mitigating disruptions and maintaining the safety and satisfaction of passengers.

news.headline: Justin Boots introduces Drennan 7" Lace-Up Roper, a modern twist on classic Western footwear.
news.summary: In Fort Worth, Texas, on March 21, 2025, Justin Boots, renowned for Western footwear innovation since 1879 and credited with creating the roper boot in the 1950s, has unveiled the Drennan 7" Lace-Up Roper. This contemporary twist on a Western staple is tailored for men seeking a blend of style and utility.

news.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) faces resistance below $87,500. ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, ADA, DOGE, TON, LINK, LEO prices analyzed. Bullish scenarios discussed.
news.summary: Bitcoin's recovery faces resistance at higher levels, with "Spoofy the Whale" suppressing prices below $87,500. BitMEX co-founder predicts a possible bottom at $77,000 due to Federal Reserve's easing measures. BlackRock's Robbie Mitchnick sees a recession as a bullish catalyst for Bitcoin. BTC may rally above $87,500, boosting altcoins. Ether struggles at $2,067 level, while XRP, BNB, Solana, Cardano, Dogecoin, Toncoin, Chainlink, and UNUS SED LEO face various resistance levels. Technical analyses suggest potential price movements for each cryptocurrency. Bitcoin may rise to $100,000 if it breaks above $87,500.ETH may fall to $1,550 if it dips below $1,927. XRP could drop to $2.22 and ADA might decline to $0.50. Dogecoin faces resistance at $0.18 and could rise to $0.29. TON may climb to $5.50 if it breaks above $4, while Chainlink could target $19.25. LEO could aim for $12.04 on a breakout above $9.90. This analysis provides insights into potential price movements in the cryptocurrency market, but readers should conduct their research before making investment decisions.

news.headline: Crypto lending market bounces back after downturn as traditional & blockchain firms inject capital. Cantor Fitzgerald's $2B Bitcoin lending business, Blockstream's funds, Xapo Bank's $1M Bitcoin loans signal renewed interest. Big institutions lead the revival.
news.summary: Digital asset lending is experiencing a resurgence as traditional financial institutions and blockchain-native companies are re-entering the market to meet the increasing demand for liquidity and leverage within the crypto sector. In the past month, major players like Cantor Fitzgerald have initiated a Bitcoin lending business supported by $2 billion in capital, while Blockstream and Xapo Bank have also secured significant capital for their crypto lending initiatives. Xapo Bank is now offering Bitcoin-collateralized loans of up to $1 million. This time around, larger institutions are leading the charge, with more banks gearing up to provide financing solutions for major players in the crypto space. During the 2021 bull market, firms like Genesis, BlockFi, and Celsius provided unsecured loans to trading desks and hedge funds.

news.headline: The UK Government is weighing plans to reduce or even abolish its digital services tax before April 2nd.
STRATA Skin Sciences to report Q4 and year end 2024 financial results on March 27, 2025 after market close. (Numbers not included)
news.summary: A medical technology company headquartered in Horsham, Pennsylvania, known as STRATA Skin Sciences, Inc. (NASDAQ: SSKN) will be releasing its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024 on March 27, 2025. The company focuses on creating and selling advanced products for treating dermatologic conditions. The report is scheduled to be disclosed after the market closes on that day. This news was shared on March 21, 2025.

news.headline: Legislation for stablecoin advancing in the House Committee, key numbers undisclosed.
Surgical robotic system market to hit $16.82B by 2034, showing 11.54% CAGR. Valued at $5.06 billion in 2023 - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
news.summary: In Dublin, the global surgical robotic system market is anticipated to expand substantially as per a report from ResearchAndMarkets.com. Figures for 2023 suggest a market value of USD 5.06 billion, with projections estimating a rise to USD 16.82 billion by 2034. This growth pattern is expected to be characterized by a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 11.54% from 2024 to 2034.

news.headline: Trump admin proposes blockchain integration to revamp USAID, improve foreign aid tracking. Aim is to boost transparency, accountability.
news.summary: The Trump administration is proposing a revamp of USAID using blockchain technology to enhance transparency and oversight in foreign aid distribution. The plan advocates for blockchain to secure and track disbursements, incentivizing aid partners to focus on outcomes rather than activities. The specifics of the technology's architecture are yet to be disclosed. This reform is part of a wider effort to address inefficiencies within USAID, which is responsible for delivering US foreign aid globally. The proposal is aimed at strengthening the effectiveness of aid programs.

news.headline: Lexington, home to No. 3 seed University of Kentucky, wins 2025 housing market tournament. Top market among others.
news.summary: In Columbia, Missouri on March 21, 2025, the University of Kentucky Wildcats are set to participate in the upcoming men's college basketball tournament as a No. 3 seed. However, amidst this anticipation, the city of Lexington is basking in glory as it emerges as the leading housing market for the year. With a No. 3 seed, the Wildcats are gearing up for the tournament, while Lexington stands out for its robust housing market performance.

news.headline: Erdogan clashes with protesters in Turkey, tensions escalate.
New owner Bret Lyman joins Always Best Care Senior Services franchises in Provo, St. George to address growing senior care needs in Utah.
news.summary: A nurse entrepreneur in Utah, Bret Lyman, is addressing the increasing demand for senior care in the state. Always Best Care Senior Services, a prominent senior care franchise in the US, revealed that Lyman has assumed a key role in meeting this need. The announcement was made on March 21, 2025, in Provo. This move comes as the senior population continues to grow, with a significant rise in seniors requiring assistance. Lyman's initiative signifies a proactive response to this trend, aiming to enhance the quality of care for seniors in Utah.

news.headline: MicroAlgo Inc. in Shenzhen plans to issue new shares at $0.8 each after convertible bond purchase agreement in 2024.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, in Shenzhen, China, MicroAlgo Inc. (NASDAQ: MLGO) disclosed its intention to offer additional shares. This decision stems from the company's agreement on October 7, 2024, to acquire convertible bonds from creditors. This move indicates a strategic shift for MicroAlgo Inc. as they aim to bolster their financial standing and potentially expand their operations. The precise number of shares to be issued was not specified in the announcement.

news.headline: Volta Finance Limited reported Net Asset Value as of February 28, 2025 for VTA / VTAS.
news.summary: The monthly report for Volta Finance Limited (VTA / VTAS) in February 2025 has been released. It is important to note that the information contained in the report is not intended for release, distribution, or publication, whether in whole or in part, in or into the United States.

news.headline: ABO-Group announces capital reduction payout to shareholders.
Check out nine new products for a fresh spring look, recommended by Bloomberg.
Senate to question Jonathan McKernan on leading CFPB amidst legitimacy crisis; focus on CFPB's future.
news.summary: Jonathan McKernan will be questioned by the Senate Banking Committee on Thursday for his confirmation hearing to head the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. The concern will likely be more on the future of the CFPB rather than on him.

news.headline: Analysis questions whether Trump's tax cuts could be cost-free to the government.implications of preventing tax increases.
news.summary: Is it costing the government trillions of dollars or nothing to prevent tax increases? This question may seem tricky, but it's worth considering.

news.headline: Trump's speech to Congress lacked focus on fellow Republicans, setting a go-it-alone vision for America.
news.summary: During his latest address to a joint session of Congress after resuming the presidency, Donald Trump notably avoided discussing his fellow Republicans, marking a departure from his usual rhetoric.

news.headline: Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency is cutting government contracts and workforces at federal agencies.
news.summary: Elon Musk's Government Efficiency Department is significantly reducing government contracts and workforces at politically sensitive federal agencies.

news.headline: Stock market experiences broad sell-off due to concerns over Trump's trade war, impacting both stocks and crypto assets.
news.summary: The financial center known as Wall Street continued to display a pessimistic outlook as concerns regarding President Donald Trump's trade conflict intensified, leading to a significant decline in both stock prices and volatile cryptocurrency assets.

news.headline: S&P 500 drops into correction territory due to Trump's tariff threats, impacting wine and whiskey budgets.
news.summary: On Thursday, the S&P 500 experienced a sharp decline, pushing it into correction territory. This drop was triggered by President Trump's announcement of potential new tariffs that could impact the prices of wine and whiskey.

news.headline: Investors disappointed as Scott Bessent fails to moderate Trump's trade policies, idea dismissed by Treasury secretary.
news.summary: The Treasury secretary is firmly upholding Trump’s stringent trade policies, disappointing Wall Street investors who had expected Scott Bessent to advocate for moderation.

news.headline: Fed's quarterly forecast for the economy to be released soon, amid clashes with Trump's agenda.
news.summary: The upcoming quarterly forecast for the economy by Federal Reserve officials is poised to clash with President Donald Trump's agenda. The announcement is scheduled for Wednesday afternoon and is eagerly awaited by market analysts. This report will provide updated economic projections, including important figures like GDP growth, unemployment rates, and inflation expectations. President Trump's policies and actions will likely impact the projections presented in the forecast, adding an element of uncertainty to the market outlook.

news.headline: Trump scraps plan to double Canadian metal tariffs from 25% to 50% after Ontario cancels charge on U.S. electricity.
news.summary: After threatening to impose a 50 percent tariff on Canadian metals, the president decided against it when Ontario removed a fee on U.S. electricity.

news.headline: Trump's new tariffs stir apprehension among investors, causing the stock market to plummet, leaving many feeling uncertain.
news.summary: President Trump caused market turmoil on Tuesday with the announcement of new tariffs on Canada. This led to a significant decline in the stock market, undermining investor confidence in its stability.

news.headline: 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum by President Trump ignite global trade conflicts, safeguarding US economy.
news.summary: The President of the United States has implemented a 25% tariff on imported metals, leading to tensions in the global trade arena. This move is aimed at protecting the American economy from international rivals.

news.headline: Timeline: Trump's tariff battle with Canada, Mexico, China, and E.U. - in 50 days of second term, market turmoil and partner confusion.
news.summary: During the initial 50 days of his second term, President Trump's actions of imposing and subsequently halting tariffs have unsettled markets and confused trading allies.

news.headline: President escalates trade tensions with allies, risking global trade wars amid threats and retaliations.
news.summary: There is a looming possibility of trade wars intensifying with allies, as the president attempts to compel trading partners to ease up on retaliatory measures by issuing fresh threats. This escalating situation could lead to significant economic consequences, potentially impacting billions of dollars in international trade. The president's strategy involves using leverage to pressure allies into changing their trade policies, furthering tensions in the global economic landscape.
